data.content: I can see the project — the express test expects `15.00m` but `CalculateShipping` returns `10.00m`. Let me start `dotnet watch test` so tests re-run automatically on every file save.

toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.description: Start dotnet watch test with hot reload

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Start dotnet watch test with hot reload

data.reasoningText:
The user wants to use `dotnet watch test` to get hot reload for their tests. Let me start it up for them. I can see that the express test will fail (expects 15.00m but the method returns 10.00m).

Let me start `dotnet watch test` in async mode so they can iterate.

arguments.description: Start dotnet watch test with hot reload

toolCalls.name: bash
toolCalls.args: {"command": "cd /tmp/sv-a68086e0cd024b6690347b53ba420af5 && dotnet watch test --no-restore 2>&1", "description": "Start dotnet watch test with hot reload", "mode": "async", "shellId": "watch"}

data.content:
`dotnet watch test` is now running. Here's the current status:

- ✅ **Passed**: `CalculateShipping_StandardDelivery_Returns5`
- ❌ **Failed**: `CalculateShipping_ExpressDelivery_Returns15` — expects `15.00` but gets `10.00`

The watcher is live in shell `watch2`. Every time you save `ShippingCalculatorTests.cs`, tests will automatically re-run. For example, changing `"express" => 10.00m` to `"express" => 15.00m` on line 24 would fix the failing test.
